title: ranunculus angustifolius subsp. uniflorus
intro: Ranunculus angustifolius subsp. uniflorus, also known as the one-flowered narrow-leaved buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of rheumatism. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the root and leaves of Ranunculus angustifolius subsp. uniflorus are typically used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures.
